title: Sunrun's VPP Output in 2024 Rivals a Fossil Fuel Power Plant
text: Sunrun's virtual power plant network achieved combined instantaneous peak capacity of nearly 80 MW across US power grids in 2024—exceeding capacity of many traditional fossil fuel plants. The network engaged over 20,000 customers across 16 VPP programs in nine states and territories, with customer batteries delivering stored solar energy across hundreds of critical grid events. California's CalReady program delivered over 16,000 customers providing average of 48 MW during summer peak evening hours, peaking at 54 MW—enough to serve approximately 48,000 homes. Puerto Rico's PowerOn Puerto Rico included more than 4,000 participants providing backup energy during 70+ energy shortfall events, helping prevent rolling blackouts. Texas partnership with Tesla Electric and Vistra provided customers $400 annual payments per Powerwall while Sunrun generated recurring revenue. New York operated over 300 solar-plus-storage systems supporting Orange & Rockland Utilities during peak summer demand. Maryland's bidirectional EV-to-home VPP launched with BGE, utilizing Ford F-150 Lightning trucks for grid support. Sunrun's leadership emphasized that with over half of new Sunrun customers installing storage, the company is laying strong foundation to create future programs where there is value for customers, benefit to grid, and revenue for Sunrun.
